reasoning: We base our estimates on the baseline 30-year Treasury yield of 4.70% (the last available value before 2025-11-09). The FRED trend analysis estimates a 30% chance of the yield exceeding this threshold by mid-November, as the broader multi-month trend has been pointing downward despite a brief recent spike. Long Treasury yields are currently elevated, but typical mean reversion from this local high gives a lower probability of further immediate increases. Consequently, the short-term probability (2025-11-16) is well below 50% at 34%. For the longer horizons (2025-12-09 and 2026-02-07), uncertainty naturally increases. Factors like persistent inflation, high term premiums, and debt pressures suggest yields could eventually rebound or stay elevated, pulling the probabilities for the later dates progressively closer to 50% (41% and 46%, respectively).
